reforms.description: The Hungarian government has implemented several initiatives to support the startup ecosystem, including the Funding for Growth Scheme (FGS) which provides liquidity at zero interest to credit institutions contingent on lending to SMEs, and the FGS+ scheme that extends credit to riskier borrowers. Additionally, a start-up incentive programme was introduced to assist young entrepreneurs with training, mentoring, and grants for initial costs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These initiatives are designed to enhance access to finance for startups and SMEs, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The FGS and start-up incentive programme have been successfully rolled out and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at bolstering the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The start-up incentive programme aims to support young people to set up their businesses through provision of entrepreneurial training, mentoring and development of business plans.

reforms.description: The Hungarian government has introduced a new public procurement framework to ensure compliance with EU rules, aiming to enhance transparency and efficiency in the use of EU structural funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving public procurement processes can lead to better allocation of resources and increased efficiency in public spending.
reforms.status_evidence: The framework has been announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more about procedural improvements rather than a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government has introduced a new public procurement framework, which should ensure that all public procurement follows EU rules in this area.

reforms.description: The government has initiated the Digital Hungary Programme to stimulate broadband penetration and achieve country-wide broadband coverage by 2018.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing broadband infrastructure is crucial for supporting innovation and digital economy growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been initiated with a target completion year.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for improving digital infrastructure but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s effort to have country-wide broadband coverage by 2018 through the Digital Hungary Programme to stimulate broadband penetration.

reforms.description: The Hungarian government has initiated a programme of administrative simplifications aimed at reducing the administrative burden of procedures and processing times for businesses, particularly in the service sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing administrative burdens can enhance business efficiency and encourage innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully rolled out and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant but does not represent a major overhaul of the innovation support system.
reforms.source_quote: The government has instigated a programme of administrative simplifications that have reduced by a quarter the administrative burden of procedures and average processing time.

reforms.description: An apprenticeship programme has been introduced to foster the employment of young qualified career starters by subsidising their wages and social contributions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to enhance the skills of young workers, thereby contributing to a more skilled workforce and supporting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully rolled out and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important for addressing youth employment, it is not a major systemic reform.
reforms.source_quote: An apprenticeship programme fosters the employment of young qualified career starters by fully or partially subsidising their wages and social contributions for nine months.
